question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTokenRelationships()] Can an unprivileged external user query GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/tokens for any arbitrary account ID they do not own, because getTokenRelationships() performs no caller identity or ownership check before invoking EntityService.getEncodedId() and TokenService.getTokenAccounts(), causing full disclosure of another account's token associations, balances, freeze_status, and kyc_status without any signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTokenRelationships()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all token relationships of a victim account by supplying the victim's numeric account ID in the path parameter, given that isValidAccount() only checks entity existence and not requester ownership, allowing the attacker to learn which tokens the victim holds and their balances without the victim's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTokenRelationships()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the isValidAccount() guard by supplying a valid but unrelated account ID in the path, since isValidAccount() returns true for any existing entity regardless of who is making the request, causing the attacker to receive token relationship data for accounts they have no signing authority over?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTokenRelationships()] Can an unprivileged external user resolve a victim's EVM address to their internal encoded account ID via EntityService.getEncodedId(), then use that ID to retrieve the victim's full token portfolio without the victim's knowledge or approval, because no ownership assertion is made after ID resolution?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTokenRelationships()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a victim's account alias in the path parameter, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to resolve it to the victim's internal ID and then return all token relationships for that account, effectively authorizing a data-access transaction against the victim's account without any signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractTokensRelationshipQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with multiple token.id=eq:X query parameters to build a large inConditions array, causing TokenService.getInClauseSubQuery() to interpolate all values directly into the SQL IN clause without parameterization, leading to SQL injection that bypasses the account_id = $1 ownership filter and returns token data for accounts the attacker does not own?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractTokensRelationshipQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user inject SQL through the token.id filter value when the operator is eq, because TokenService.getInClauseSubQuery() constructs the IN clause as a raw string interpolation (in (${tokenIdInParams})) without using parameterized placeholders, allowing the attacker to escape the query and read or modify token_account rows belonging to other owners?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractTokensRelationshipQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=gt:0 filter to retrieve all token associations for a victim account starting from token ID 0, bypassing any intended scoping, because the conditions array is appended directly to the base query that already fixes account_id = $1 but does not verify the requester is the account owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractTokensRelationshipQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=lt:999999999 filter to retrieve all token associations below a large token ID for a victim account, since the conditions array is built without any ownership check on the requester, causing full disclosure of the victim's token holdings without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractTokensRelationshipQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=gte:1 filter combined with a large limit to dump the entire token_account table rows for a victim account in a single request, because extractTokensRelationshipQuery() places no restriction on who can specify range conditions for any ownerAccountId?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTokenRelationships()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any HTTP authentication middleware enforcement in getTokenRelationships() to access token relationship data for high-value accounts (e.g., exchange wallets), since authHandler.js only sets a custom rate limit for authenticated users but does not block unauthenticated access to this endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTokenRelationships()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the freeze_status field in the TokenRelationshipViewModel response for a victim account's tokens without being a freeze key holder or signer, because getTokenRelationships() returns freeze_status for all token associations of any account without verifying the requester's authority?
